author: The Works of John Cassian
book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
Chapter XXX. He adds also S. John Chrysostom.

source.title: NPNF-211. Sulpitius Severus, Vincent of Lerins, John Cassian

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: As for John the glory of the Episcopate of Constantinople, whose holy life obtained the reward of martyrdom without any show of Gentile persecution, hear what he thought and taught on the Incarnation of the Son of God: “And Him,” he says, “whom if He had come in unveiled Deity neither the heaven nor the earth nor the sea nor any other creature could have contained, the pure womb of a Virgin bore.”

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: This man’s faith and doctrine then, even if you ignore that of others, you ought to follow and hold, as out of love and affection for him the pious people chose you as their Bishop.

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: For when it took you for its priest from the Church of Antioch, from which it had formerly chosen him, it believed that it would receive in you all that it had lost in him.

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: Did not, I ask you, all these almost with prophetic spirit say all these things in order to confound your blasphemies.

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: For you declare that our Lord and Saviour Christ is not God: they declare that Christ the Lord is Very God.

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: You blasphemously assert that Mary is Christotocos not Theotocos: they do not deny that she is Christotocos, while they acknowledge her as Theotocos.

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: Not merely the substance but the words also are opposed to your blasphemies: that we may clearly see that an impregnable bulwark was formerly prepared by God against your blasphemies, to break on the wall of truth ready prepared, the force of the heretical attack which was at some time or other to come.

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: And you, O you most wicked and shameless contaminator of an illustrious city, you disastrous and deadly plague of a Catholic and holy people, do you dare to stand and teach in the Church of God, and with your wild and blasphemous words slander the priests of an ever unbroken faith and Catholic confession, and say that the people of the city of Constantinople are in error through the fault of their earlier teachers?

verses.book: The Seven Books of John Cassian on the Incarnation of the Lord, Against Nestorius.
verses.text: Are you then the corrector of former Bishops, the accuser of ancient priests, are you better than Gregory, more approved than Nectarius, greater than John, and all the other Bishops of Eastern cities who, though not of the same renown as those whom I have enumerated, were yet of the same faith? which, as far as the matter in hand is concerned, is enough: for when it is a question of the faith, all are as good as the best in so far as they agree with the best.
